other_observers: Brandon Percival; I was standing next to Brandon when he found it.
description: loon with thin bill pointed upwards some of the time. This bird was in bad light and far away but there were Common Loons nearby for size and shape comarison. This is based on the jizz, size, and shape, especially the bill. I took photographs but the heat shimmer kept photographs from being diagnostic although scope view were. Brandon Percival found the bird and we tried to photograph and digiscope it but were mostly unsuccessful with that endeavor.Behaviors: Actively feeding, diving and under surface for as much as 25 seconds at a time. In the middle of the reserovoir with large greebes, mergansers, and Common Loons nearby.Call: none heardPlumage: first winter
